'''Marvin Golden''' (born 21 December 1976) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club for [[Hunslet Parkside A.R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Parkside]] [[Hunslet Parkside ARLFC|ARLFC]], the [[Leeds Rhinos]] including in [[Super League I|1996]]'s, [[Super League II|1997]]'s, [[Super League III|1998]]'s and [[Super League IV|1999]]'s [[Super League]], [[Bramley RLFC|Bramley]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] in [[Super League VI|2001]]'s [[Super League]], the [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster Dragons]]  and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in [[Super League VIII|2003]]'s [[Super League]], he also played for [[Illawarra Steelers]] (non-[[First Grade]]) in Australia, as a {{rlp|wg}}, or {{rlp|ce}}.

==Background==
Marvin Golden was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, he now works for TRAD Safety Systems located in [[Morley, West Yorkshire|Morley]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.
